title: Week 2 Seminar Slides
body:
INFS 3822
 Artificial Intelligence for Business Analytics




Week 2 Seminar

AI-Driven Business Analytics


        Dr Jason Xianghua Wu
        School of Information Systems & Technology Management
        UNSW Business School
 Week 2 Seminar Outline

                               AI-Driven Business Analytics

                 Descriptive      Diagnostic      Predictive      Prescriptive


            Practical       Predictive Data Analytics
            Example         to Improve Customer Service


                          AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

                 Understand      Translate   Prepare    Analyse    Deploy        Generate



                          AI-Driven Business Analytics in Practice

                              Marketing & Sales                   Transportation Sector




                        AI for Personalised Marketing      AI for Autonomous Transport
   Reflect on your personal experience
Picture these scenarios
1. You are a high school student deciding which University to attend

2. Course selection and academic performance


3. Internship and Job Search


4. You are tracking your income and expenses over the past 6 months


                                                                       3
 Types of Business Analytics
                         Descriptive Analytics
                         Provide insights into the past
                   Answer the question “What has happened?”
  Applications
  • Retail: Analyse historical sales data to identify best-selling/most returned products,
    changes in consumer purchase behaviour
  • Marketing: Examine website traffic data (average number daily visitors, average
    session duration, returned visitor rate, click-through rate)
  • Healthcare: Analyse patients’ health records to assess the success rates of certain
    procedures, patient recovery times, or post-surgery complications
  • Manufacturing: Investigate historical production data to determine the number of
    defective units, defect rates per batch, and issues within specific product lines
                                                                                             4
 Types of Business Analytics
                            Descriptive Analytics




    The importance of descriptive analytics using dashboards
            Source: https://youtu.be/KBpFy1zY1mI               5
 Types of Business Analytics

                        Diagnostic Analytics
                  Analyse past data to determine the causes
                 Answer the question “Why did this happen?”
  Applications
  • Retail: Understand the key factors contributing a decline in online sales
  • Marketing: Understand the key factors influencing bounce rate
  • Healthcare: Understand the reasons behind patient readmissions (patients who
    return after discharge)
  • Manufacturing: Identify key factors causing production delays (e.g. equipment
    malfunctions, supply chain disruptions, or operator errors)



                                                                                    6
 Types of Business Analytics

                         Predictive Analytics
             Predict what will happen, and why it will happen,
         Answer the question “What might happen in the future?”
  Applications
  • Retail: Understand the key factors contributing a decrease in online sales revenue
     ➢ Forecasting future sales trends and customer behaviour
  • Marketing: Understand the key factors contributing to customer churn
  • Healthcare: Understand the reasons behind patient readmissions (patients who
    return shortly after discharge)
     ➢ Predicting which patients at risk of being readmitted
  • Manufacturing: Understand the key factors contributing to production delays
     ➢ Predicting equipment malfunctions, supply chain disruptions
                                                                                         7
 Types of Business Analytics

                        Prescriptive Analytics
                        Advise on possible outcomes
                   Answer the question “What should we do?”
Applications
• Retail: Identify the key factors contributing a decrease in online sales revenue
   ➢ Develop strategies to address these factors and optimise sales performance
• Human Resources: Understand the key drivers of employee attrition
   ➢ Provide recommendations to reduce employee turnover and improve retention
• Healthcare: Determine the reasons behind patient readmissions after discharge
   ➢ Suggest interventions to reduce readmission rates/ improve patient health
• Manufacturing: Understand the key factors contributing to production delays
   ➢ Recommend the best course of action to minimise production delays

                                                                                     8
  AI-driven Business Analytics
                  Application to the supply chain sector


                                                          Predictive Analytics to Improve
                                                        Customer Service in the supply chain
                                                                      sector

                                                                                                                     9
                            Let’s learn how to apply AI/ML
                           to conduct Business Analytics




AI-Driven Business Analytics Process




                                                            10
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process
 1   Understand the Business Problem

 2   Translate the Business Problem into a Machine Learning Problem

 3   Prepare Relevant Data (collect, clean, transform)

 4   Analyse the Data (training, testing)

 5   Deploy The best performing ML model for Inference

 6   Generate Actionable Insights from the findings

                                                                      11
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

    1   Understand the Business Problem

•   Gain a deeper understanding of the problem you are trying to solve
•   Formulate the problem statement by clearly articulating what issue or
    challenge the business is facing
•   How-to: Apply Design Thinking Framework
•   Define the objective and articulate the expected outcomes




                                                                            12
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

        1   Understand the Business Problem

•       Identify the business problem and clearly define the business goal
•       Frame the problem within its context
    •       Apply 2 Design thinking frameworks (PEST analysis, CSF analysis)
•       Find the underlying causes of the problem
    •       (5W 1H? What, Who, Why, When, Where and How to determine the key drivers/factors)
•       Discuss key proposed solution to address the problem: Application of ML?
•       Frame a problem statement



                                                                                                13
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

     1   Understand the Business Problem: Customer Churn

•    Clearly articulate the business problem!

    Picture the following scenario
    "Our customer churn rate has been increasing steadily over the past six
    months. We need to develop a ML model to identify the key factors leading to
    customer churn and predict which customers are at the highest risk of
    leaving”.

         ➢ To determine targeted retention strategies to reduce customer churn!


                                                                                  14
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

     1    Understand the Business Problem: Customer Churn

• Brainstorming!
     Frame the problem within the context
 •       Apply the 5 W1H approach to frame the problem within the context
         Who What Where When Why and How the problem?
 •       What is churn?
         Clearly define what customer churn means for your business?
 •       Why is churn a problem?
 •       What are the implications?
 •       What is the cost to the company if this problem continues?
         The cost of re-acquiring churned customers vs. retaining existing ones
         Customers who churned have lost trust in the company. Is it challenging to regain trust?



                                                                                                    15
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

     1    Understand the Business Problem: Customer Churn

• Brainstorming in groups!
     Frame the problem within the context
 •       What are the potential causes of the problem?
 •       Why do customers decide to churn?
 •       What are the early signs of customer churn?
 •       What are the Key factors contributing to customer churn?
 •       Where is churn happening?
         Identify the stages in the customer journey where churn is likely to occur
 •       Where are the churn-prone customers located?
         Geographical locations, specific devices (mobile vs. desktop), or channels (online vs. in-store)


                                                                                                            16
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

     1   Understand the Business Problem: Customer Churn

• Brainstorming in groups!
     Problem statement!
 •       Clearly articulate the problem you are trying to solve
 •       Think of possible solutions to address this problem
 •       How can we apply ML to reduce customer churn?




                                                                  17
     AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

          1   Understand the Business Problem: Customer Churn

    •      Apply relevant design thinking frameworks
                                             PEST Analysis
        Political Factors           Economic Factors        Social Factors         Technological
                                                                                      Factors
•       Consumer Protection     •   Recession            • Lifestyle changes   • Slow loading times
        Regulations             •   High/Low income        (e.g. consuming     • Poor navigation
          • Return Policies     •   GDP growth             health-conscious    • Frequent downtimes
          • Warranty
                                •   Inflation rate         products)           • Data breaches (e.g.
          • False Advertising
•       Non-compliance or       •   Unemployment level   • Culture               Optus, Medibank)
        perceived unfair                                                       • Privacy Concerns
        practice can lead to
        customer churn
                                                                                                       18
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

     2     Translate the Business Problem into a Machine Learning Problem

• Formulate the problem that needs to be solved with ML
 •       Convert the business problem into a ML problem
 •       Clearly outline the ML problem
 •       Explain how application ML technique contributes to address the business problem
          • Predict the key drivers of customer churn?
• Apply design thinking frameworks
 •       Business Model Canvas (BMC)
 •       Opportunity Canvas to justify the application of ML
• Articulate the expected outcomes (opportunities for improvements)
 •       What is the value/benefit for the company?


                                                                                            19
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

   2   Translate the Business Problem into a Machine Learning Problem

• Determine what type of ML technique is most suitable to address the business problem

 • Supervised Learning
       • Is it Classification, Regression, Or Ranking problem?

 • Unsupervised Learning
       • Is it Clustering, Anomaly Detection, or Recommendation Problem?

 ✓ How do you determine the most suitable ML algorithms?
 ✓ What strategy should you implement to select the best ML algorithm?
 ✓ How do you assess the performance of the ML models?


                                                                                         21
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

    3   Prepare Relevant Data (collect, clean, transform)

•   Data is the fuel of the ML algorithm
•   Data is key factor in ML model performance

•   Two types of data
                  Structured data             Unstructured data




                                                                  22
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

        3   Prepare Relevant Data (collect, clean, transform)

•       AI-Driven Business Analytics require data that is:
    •       Large -Big data is best for AI-Driven solutions to a business problem
    •       Consistently and reliably collected e.g. data gathered over many years from the
            same reliable sources
    •       From trusted sources
    •       Correct/clean- Data may require cleaning/transformation to ensure that it is free
            from incorrect values
    •       Representative- The datasets should be as representative of the real world as
            possible e.g. You cannot train a ML model using fake data
    •       The features in your dataset should have predictive power

 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

     3   Prepare Relevant Data (collect, clean, transform)

•    Why data quality matters?
    Picture the following scenario!
    In 2014, Amazon developed an experimental ML driven recruitment tool. Similar to
    Amazon’s rating system, the hiring tool was designed to evaluate job applicants by
    assigning scores ranging from 1 to 5 stars when screening resumes to identify the
    best candidates.
    Amazon discontinued its secret AI recruiting tool due to gender bias “The company
    realised that its new system was not evaluating candidates for software developer
    jobs and other technical posts in a gender-neutral manner” (Reuters, 2018)



                                                                                         25
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

     3   Prepare Relevant Data (collect, clean, transform)

•    How much data is needed?
    • Construct a training dataset
    • How much data is enough to train a good model?
    • Ensure the data is sufficient for training the ML model
      • Large, Diverse, Representative, High Quality
    • There is no formula to help calculate the right size of dataset for a ML
      model
                           𝐷𝑎𝑡𝑎 𝑆𝑖𝑧𝑒 = 𝑓(𝑥)

                                                                                 27
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

     3    Prepare Relevant Data (collect, clean, transform)

•    How is data prepared for ML?
    • Ensure not only high-quality data
    • But also, its adequacy to set task!
    • The chosen data must be transformed into a format that is most suitable
      for the model
    • Importance of data preparation!

                        Data
                     Preparation


         Data                        Training Dataset   ML Algorithm   ML Model   28
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

        3       Prepare Relevant Data (collect, clean, transform)

•    Why Data Cleaning is important?
    • Enhance data quality
            •     Clean data ensures the data fed into the model is accurate, which directly impacts the
                  model's performance
            •     Inaccurate data lead to misleading conclusions and unreliable models
    •       Handle Missing Values
            •     Identify and address missing values,
            •     To ensure the dataset is complete and prevents the introduction of bias in the model
                  training process
    •       Correct Errors in the data
            •     Detect and address any inconsistencies or errors
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

        3       Prepare Relevant Data (collect, clean, transform)

•    Why Data Cleaning is important?
    • Facilitate Feature Engineering
            •     Clean data provides a solid foundation to create new features or modify existing ones,
                  which can significantly enhance model performance
    •       Reduce Overfitting
            •     Eliminate irrelevant or redundant features to reduce overfitting,
            •     The model performs well on training data but poorly on new, unseen data
    •       Enhance Model Selection
            •     Clean data allows for a more accurate comparison of different ML algorithms, as the
                  models are evaluated on a consistent and high-quality dataset
            •     Help in deciding the algorithms are best suited for the given problem based on the quality
                  and characteristics of the cleaned data
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

     3     Prepare Relevant Data (collect, clean, transform)

 •        Understand Data Distribution
         • Identify relationships between features, underlying patterns, trends
         • Detect outliers that might affect the ML model’s performance
         • Identify potential key predictors
         • Feature selection
            •   Understand the significance of various features, to select the most relevant ones
                for the model
            •   Remove irrelevant or redundant features to improve model efficiency/
                performance
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

  4   Analyse the Data (training, testing)

Determine which ML Algorithm is suitable
• Problem type
• Characteristics of the data
• Why don’t we try all the ML algorithms and select
  the best performing model?
 • Time consuming!
 • What about data visualisation?
 • Pair Plot to decide which ML algorithm you should
   apply

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      33
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

   4   Analyse the Data (training, testing)

ML Training
Train the ML model using historical data to enable it to learn patterns and relationships
• Divide data into training and testing sets: Cross Validation
 • Typically, 80% for training and 20% for testing
 • Randomly selected. Why?
• Beware of Overfitting!
 • When the model learns the training data too well, including noise and outliers




                                                                                        34
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

  4   Analyse the Data (training, testing)

ML Testing
• Assess the ML model’s performance to make accurate predictions
• Performance metrics include:
 • Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1 Score, and Mean Squared Error
• Testing dataset must be different from the training dataset to evaluate the
  model’s ability to perform well on new, unseen data




                                                                                35
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

     4     Analyse the Data (training, testing)

ML model Evaluation
• Confusion Matrix
 •       Evaluate the performance of a classification model by showing the True Positives, True Negatives,
         False Positives, False Negatives → Understand the errors made by the model
• ROC Curve and AUC (Area Under the Curve)
 •       Representation of a classifier’s performance across all classification thresholds to select the optimal
         model and threshold
 •       Higher AUC indicates better model performance
• Choose the best performing ML model
 •       Select the model with the best optimal combination of accuracy, precision, recall, and AUC


                                                                                                               36
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

        5   Deploy The best performing ML model for Inference

•       Deploy the ML model for real-time predictions or decisions, accessible to internal
        and external stakeholders
                           Training → Testing → Inference (predictions)
•       Deployment types
    •       Cloud platforms
            AWS SageMaker, Google AI platform, Microsoft Azure ML, Huggingface (we use it today in hands-on activity)
    •       Edge Devices
            Smartphones, IoT devices, Autonomous Vehicles
    •       Embedded Systems
            Home Appliances, Medical Devices
•       Refine the ML model by further training with new, unseen data

                                                                                                                        37
 AI-Driven Business Analytics Process

    6   Generate Actionable Insights from the findings

•   Discuss the findings generated by the ML model
•   Derive actionable insights
•   Translate the actionable insights into recommendations to address the
    business problem
•   Implement the recommendations and monitor the impact on the business
    performance
•   Continuously track the performance to assess the effectiveness of the
    solutions and make necessary adjustments or refinement

 Artificial Intelligence in Transportation

• Application: Autonomous vehicles, including
 •   Cars (e.g. Tesla FSD – Full Self Drive)
 •   Autonomous ships
 •   Autonomous drones
• Type of ML models
 •   Deep learning
 •   Swarm-based fuzzy controllers
 •   Wireless sensors
 •   Actuator networks
• Case study:

 Artificial Intelligence in Transportation

                               4 days truck trip                 Remaining shelf life
                         (including driver rest time)                 of 10 days
Shelf life of 14 days                                              in cold storage

  1   Understand the Business Problem




                       Actual driving is 40 hours
                       Regulations impose driver
                              resting time




                                                    Source: https://youtu.be/6-02xycYbws   42
 Artificial Intelligence in Transportation

    2   Translate the Business Problem into a Machine Learning Problem

•   Autonomous driving enables a truck to operate continuously for 40 hours
•   Is GPS navigation enough to enable autonomy? No, it must take into consideration
    the existing traffic and dynamic road conditions
•   Can cameras be mounted on trucks? Yes / Why?
•   Can trucks be controlled by a computer? Yes / Why?
•   Data required to train the ML model includes full recordings of trips conducted by
    human drivers (training dataset)
•   Sensors would include GPS, Vision, Lidar & Radar
•   ML model would require Vision AI and can be supervised or reinforcement learning

 Artificial Intelligence in Marketing and Sales

Market Segmentation and Targeting
• Targeted/personalised marketing is up to 14% more effective than mass
  marketing
 • Need for accurate and unbiased customer segmentation to enable targeted marketing
• AI can help in marketing segmentation by
 • Reducing human bias when placing consumers in groups
 • Uncovering hidden patterns within data
 • Automatically updating customer segments in a rapidly changing environment




                                                                                       50
 Artificial Intelligence in Marketing and Sales

Case: Starbucks using AI for Personalised Marketing
• Deep Brew AI platform generates insights into consumer preferences and offers
  personalised recommendations

                                                                                                               52
 Artificial Intelligence in Marketing and Sales
Sales and Product Pricing
•    What is the best product price?
•    Typically determined by the fundamental law of supply and demand
•    AI analyses vast amounts of data on demand, supply, competition, and sales
•    Adjust prices in real-time based on the market conditions
    • Provide personalised pricing based on customer behaviour and preferences

• Determine the optimal price for a product
   • AI models consider multiple factors and their interactions
   • Provides more accurate and optimal pricing strategies

    ➢    By setting optimal prices, businesses can maximise revenue
    ➢    Personalised pricing can enhance customer loyalty and satisfaction

 Case study
AI-based dynamic pricing of Rideshare Services at Lyft

 Week 3 Seminar: Analysing Structured Data using AI

• Structured data in the business context
• Machine Learning for Structured Data Analysis
• Pre-processing Structured Data for ML
• Deep Learning for Structured Data Analysis
• AI-Driven Structured Data Analysis to solve Business Problems
• Applications in Finance & the Credit, Lending, and Mortgage sector
• Hands-On Deep Dive Pattern recognition using Deep Learning for
  Credit Card Fraud Detection in Python / R
